programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Rocky, a young pit bull, is fighting for his life after being bitten by a vicious rattle snake. Also, Sassquatch the cat suffers after getting caught in a fire at his owner's apartment.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Tom Parker Bowles and Tony Singh, and by special guest Natalie Cassidy. Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes. There are also great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan takes Anna to the foodie capital of Ireland. Katherine's memories of her grandmother's over-boiled and under-seasoned meals have left her with a bad impression of Irish food, but Anna is on a mission to change that. First stop is the world-renowned Ballymaloe Cookery School, somewhere that inspired Anna's own culinary journey, followed by a visit to one of the oldest food markets in Europe, the English Market. Will this change Katherine's mind?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Mould... Always Believe in Your Soul!
programme.desc: The mould in a Hampshire house has Martin singing the blues, Martel needs to watch her step in County Durham, and Dion goes wild in Warrington in an overgrown garden.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp, experts Ben Cooper and Izzie Balmer and the teams go antique-hunting in Nottinghamshire, before heading to Derby for today's auction. Natasha meets a former Bargain Hunt contestant with a world record collection of bagpipes, and she has a go herself.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams is in rural Herefordshire, helping a couple who are looking to buy their first home together where they can finally get their collection of potted plants in the ground.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: They say don't walk under a ladder... Jacqui does more than that, and designer Ayo unlocks the potential of a £2 table with a bold design.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Helen and Kevin are nearing full retirement and have a garden dominated by three things - a patio that looks too big for the space, a breeze-block wall that looks ugly and a shed that sticks out like a sore thumb.

programme.title: Shrek
programme.desc: The story of a reclusive green ogre called Shrek whose life of solitude is disrupted by a horde of refugees who have been banished by the evil Lord Farquaad. Determined to save his home, Shrek cuts a deal with the tyrant, who sets him a daunting task - if Shrek is ever to enjoy peace and quiet again, he must rescue Farquaad's bride-to-be from a dangerous castle.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents a celebrity special edition of the high-stakes quiz. Rugby player Joe Marler, actor Jennie McAlpine, comedian Geoff Norcott and presenter Jean Johansson face the Bridge of Lies.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts as Alison Hammond is rudely awoken in the Midnight Gameshow, Ricky Wilson is the Unexpected Star of the Show, Anne-Marie hands over her phone in Send to All, and James Blunt performs.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Stevie reels to discover Matty covered for her mistake.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe and her family arrive on a remote Greek island, hoping a holiday reunion with friends will be an escape from reality. But can she keep pretending when her husband Dan's dark secret threatens to ruin everything? A hedonistic first night party is the release they all need. But it sets off a chain of events that will change them all forever. Zoe begins to wonder if there are bigger forces at play. We are in Greece after all, land of the gods.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night's betrayal didn't mean anything. But Dan's behaviour threatens to push Zoe over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn't know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation.

programme.title: BBC Radio 1's Big Weekend
programme.desc: Radio 1's Big Weekend returns to Sunderland's Herrington Country Park for a bank holiday weekend packed with live music. programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Perth and Kinross, helping a young couple with a budget of £350,000 to find an ideal country home for them and their two young sons. Jules also meets a group of keen stand-up paddleboarders who do their bit to keep the waterways free of litter.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Wild West, where coyotes face a snowbound Yellowstone, rattlesnakes emerge in the hundreds, woodpeckers compete to store food, bison battle on the Great Plains and cranes gather in vast numbers on their age-old migration.

programme.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru
programme.desc: In the 1970s, young Gru tries to join a group of supervillains called the Vicious 6 after they oust their legendary leader. When the interview turns disastrous, Gru and his Minions find themselves on the run with the Vicious 6 hot on their tails.

programme.title: Death Valley

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: With the England team at an all-time low after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland, Gareth Southgate becomes the new manager. With psychologist Pippa Grange, he sets about changing the team's mindset and tackling his and England's biggest fear - penalties.

First stop is the world-renowned Ballymaloe Cookery School, somewhere that inspired Anna's own culinary journey, followed by a visit to one of the oldest food markets in Europe, the English Market. Will this change Katherine's mind?

programme.sub-title: Mould... Always Believe in Your Soul!

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night's betrayal didn't mean anything. But Dan's behaviour threatens to push Zoe over the edge. programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe and her family arrive on a remote Greek island, hoping a holiday reunion with friends will be an escape from reality. But can she keep pretending when her husband Dan's dark secret threatens to ruin everything? A hedonistic first night party is the release they all need. But it sets off a chain of events that will change them all forever. Zoe begins to wonder if there are bigger forces at play. We are in Greece after all, land of the gods.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night's betrayal didn't mean anything. But Dan's behaviour threatens to push Zoe over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn't know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation.

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan takes Anna to the foodie capital of Ireland. Katherine's memories of her grandmother's over-boiled and under-seasoned meals have left her with a bad impression of Irish food, but Anna is on a mission to change that. First stop is the world-renowned Ballymaloe Cookery School, somewhere that inspired Anna's own culinary journey, followed by a visit to one of the oldest food markets in Europe, the English Market. Will this change Katherine's mind?

programme.title: Icons of Football
programme.desc: Davie Cooper holds a unique place in football folklore. This portrait of the Rangers and Motherwell legend shows the many sides to one of the greatest players of his generation.

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: It's the grand final! Interior designer Anna Campbell Jones, architect Danny Campbell and Isle of Mull-based interior designer Banjo Beale have scoured the country in search of Scotland's home of the year. It's now down to the final six homes - the winners of each regional episode - to go head-to-head, but only one of them can take the prestigious title.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: With Ken needing to re-sit his driving exam in order to win back his licence, Richard offers to take him out on a mock test, not realising just how rusty his dad has become behind the wheel.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A family attempt to save their failing funeral home in the sleepy Scottish fishing village of Kincully. Archie, having recently inherited the floundering family business from his domineering dad, is determined to prove himself.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: James is cursed with `acute misfortune syndrome'. James tries to reconnect with his dad after being thrown out by his mum for setting fire to their house at a family barbecue. In this world, chronic bad luck exists and the unbelievable is the everyday.

programme.title: Highland Cops
programme.desc: A team of police travel to the Outer Hebrides to search through the snow for a missing 16-year-old. In Shetland, the cops have to deal with Viking revellers and a drunk driver.

programme.title: Mark Walters in the Footsteps of Andrew Watson
programme.desc: Mark Walters looks back at his career with the Glasgow club to assess the racism he faced while playing for them. Watching harrowing footage of his early matches, he describes the visceral atmosphere as he was targeted by opposition fans.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Chief Commissioner Miekelson's plan for working at home do not go to plan and Traffic Officers Singh and McKirdy face a public relations nightmare over parking illegally to collect a takeaway.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Stevie persuades Miranda to move on following Gary's departure to Hong Kong, and so Miranda starts a new regime. She is determined to get fit, lose weight and become a new woman.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is a clumsy woman, but falling in a grave at the end of a funeral service was something not even she was expecting. The experience gets her thinking about what people will say at her funeral and she wants it to be good.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown's grandson Bono is being bullied by a boy at school. When Agnes makes matters worse, a mediation session is called. What could possibly go wrong?

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: After a crime wave hits Finglas, the Garda prompts Father Damien and Maria to put together a Neighbourhood Watch scheme. Agnes and Winnie promptly sign up, but are they really the best people to tackle crime in the area? Also, after Cathy goes on yet another disastrous date, Agnes is determined to put Cathy's man troubles to rest once and for all. Meanwhile, Betty and Mark are locking swords over whether Bono should go to his school disco.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: It's time for Conor and Jock to try the exciting Cork dating scene when two girls visiting the city catch their attention.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Mairéad and the lads take the family car on a road trip to pick up her unpredictable mother, but disaster strikes.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Sun and sand are the new dream, but how can the lads afford it? Enter Billy Murphy with a daring whiskey heist idea.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: There's no place like home when Dickie takes his workmates on a Wizard of Oz night out.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Mam is managing a team for a local charity football match, where Dickie and Lucinda compete to become the best WAGs!

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is mugged and decides she wants to become a Police Community Support Officer. Wesley comes back to Runcorn in an attempt to win back Donna's heart and asks Gaz for advice. Donna is furious with Gaz after he tries to set her up with a tramp, and Louise is determined to find a father figure to help her look after baby Louise. Wesley decides to try and show Donna that he's serious about her.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet has become a PCSO and tries to gain Gaz's respect, but lands herself in deep water. Louise tries to improve baby Louise's IQ by quizzing her on mathematics. Donna and Gaz decide they are not ready to have a happy divorce yet. Wesley tries to fit in with the gang by learning what he sees as the Runcornian way of life: eating pasties, shimmying like Tim and borrowing each other's clothes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna and Gaz have decided to get divorced and it takes them on a path down memory lane, looking back at their relationship in a series of fantasies and flashbacks, including the time they first met one another at the age of five. Donna wonders what she and Louise will be like as old ladies and Gaz imagines how fat he may have been if they'd never met.

programme.desc: After Pancake saves him from a mishap, Flutter vows to repay the debt. But his risk-averse behaviour drives Pan to distraction until a real emergency allows the debt to be repaid.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard is jealous of the vacuum cleaner and hides it from Dad and Cosima. Ammon decides it's her new best friend, whilst Cosima reassures Big Lizard that he will always be her BFF.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes quest on Spookyween to stop whatever is menacing the pumpkin patch and find that a headless skeleton is using pumpkins as a head.

programme.desc: Liko and Roy reunite with one of their friends from the Rising Volt Tacklers.

programme.title: Bro's in Control

programme.desc: Messy painting and a ruined celebrity masterpiece at a fake art auction.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Matilda the Musical, and The Mandalorian and Grogu

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: G.I. Blues
programme.desc: A musically-gifted GI stationed in Germany forms a group with two other soldiers while romancing a cabaret performer.

programme.title: Funny Face
programme.desc: A Madison Avenue photographer transforms a timid saleswoman into one of the fashion world's most glamorous models. The Gershwin-scored film includes `How Long Has This Been Going On?, He Loves and She Loves, and `S Wonderful'.

programme.title: WSL LIVE
programme.sub-title: Relegation Play-Off: Charlton Athletic v Leicester City
programme.desc: Coverage of the Women's Super League play-off as Charlton Athletic play Leicester City.

programme.title: Great Bear Stakeout
programme.desc: The world-famous salmon run arrives in Alaska. The bears must catch hundreds of these fish if they are to bulk up and survive the harsh winter months. We discover that young grizzly mum Parsnip and her cub Pushki have returned to the bay to feed on the salmon. Predatory adult bears will kill cubs and, with fierce competition, Parsnip must protect her cub and learn the skills of fishing.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: `Flog It!' Comes from the National Maritime Museum, Cornwall based in Falmouth. The museum explores the region's seafaring history and hosts our antiques experts Charles Hanson and Caroline Hawley.

programme.title: UEFA Women's Champions League Football LIVE
programme.sub-title: Final: FC Barcelona v OL Lyonnes
programme.desc: All the action from the Women's Champions League final as Barcelona play Lyon in Oslo.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul's loch-hopping journey starts on the west coast, on a wild and windswept stretch of Loch Hourn. Travelling inland, he reaches sheltered Loch Quoich, where he hears tales of Scotland's last outlaw. On the shores of Loch Lochy, he meets a couple who had a memorable encounter with a monster from the deep called Lizzie. A detour south leads to a ruined bridge where the first shots were fired of the '45, and Paul then walks an old railway line above the Great Glen, and inhales diesel smoke.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Rachel de Thame look back at the highlights of their week at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026, including the inspirational garden designs and horticultural excellence on display.

programme.title: Cher at the BBC
programme.desc: Turn back time on a journey through the BBC archives to celebrate singing superstar Cher and her 75th birthday. This selection of songs and chat show moments features many of the hits that have made Cher the only artist to have US number ones in each of the past six decades. Including favourites like `Believe', `Love and Understanding', `Walking in Memphis' and her first UK chart topper `I Got You Babe', recorded with ex-husband and singing partner Sonny Bono.

programme.title: Cher Meets Rylan
programme.desc: The Goddess of Pop sits down for a fun and in-depth chat with Rylan Clark about her new record, her biggest hits and her incredible life and career.

programme.title: My Week With Marilyn
programme.desc: Colin Clark works as an assistant on the set of the Laurence Olivier and Marilyn Monroe film the `Prince and the Showgirl'. When Arthur Miller, Monroe's playwright husband, leaves the country, Clark introduces Monroe to British life in a week in which she escapes from her Hollywood routine and the pressures of stardom.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 2008. After a breakdown, Niall learns that Ruben has returned. Obsession takes hold, and a charged confrontation forces the brothers to face their fractured past.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A man is found dead while he is meant to be serving community service. After some time apart, Janie and John must come together and get creative in order to crack the case.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Pawn shop owner Les hears a killer story from a group of clients. But are they to be trusted? Later, he also makes a very important discovery.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth is shocked when Les brings back ex-pawn shop employee Amy. He later spots a mistake that could cost the store thousands of dollars. Is she to blame?

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: There's trouble in the shop as a woman catches her boyfriend secretly trying to flog her ring. Plus, Les lays his hands on some priceless rock memorabilia.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Today Ilford plays host to David Dickinson and his team, where Michael Hogben can't believe his luck when a pair of Doulton figurines arrive at his table, Henry Nicholls tries his best to buy a Breitling watch and seller Mary drives a hard bargain for her collection of silver gilt coins.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: Englishman David Kay joins the auction action in Fort Worth, Victor uncovers spy gear, Ricky and Bubba try to land something large and dangerous.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The bidders compete in Dallas, Lesa and Jerry pester Mary and Jenny, cowboy Matt Blevins is targeted by Ricky and Bubba, a musical instrument's fate garners differing opinions.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The bidders return to the Dallas suburb of Carrollton, Ricky and Bubba uncover a den of deviance, a confident Kenny returns to action, Mary brings a roaring crowd to its feet.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: Cowboy Matt stirs up trouble in Fort Worth, Mary hopes to take flight with a purchase, Jenny's discovery leads to a photo opportunity, Matt puts his hand where it doesn't belong.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: Ricky and Bubba face off against Kenny and other locals in the eastern Texas city of Tyler, Jenny uncovers a holy grail of the music world and one buyer loses money before the auction starts.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The bidders look for big deals in the small town of Ponder, Jenny acts like a ballerina while Mary works the crowd and Ricky and Bubba are provoked by Cowboy Matt Blevins.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: As a new era of exploration of the Red Planet begins, the Red Planet's mysteries might be close to getting solved; ancient cataclysms are believed to have left Mars barren, but science continues to find clues that this red planet was once blue.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Some archeological finds are so baffling, they challenge what is known about the past; learning the truth about these mysterious ruins and answering the questions they continue to present.

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Millions mourned the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, but he's been spotted hundreds of times since. Experts look into whether the King of Rock and Roll is still alive.

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Exploring beliefs about where people go when they die, reincarnation and unlocking past life memories.

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: God moves in mysterious ways and sometimes appears in mysterious places. Why do so many people see a vision of Jesus in seemingly normal sights? Is it blind faith or real appearances?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Broadhaven is a sleepy little seaside village in Wales and the UFO capital of Europe. Night after night mysterious lights have been seen in the sky here.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Marble Mountain Bigfoot, Waldek Mine Whispers, and UFO Buzzes Jet
programme.desc: Is footage captured by campers at a known Bigfoot hot spot in Northern California the latest evidence that Sasquatch really exists? Did a man in Australia hear the whispers of the dead in an old abandoned gold mine?

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: ISS UFO Swarm, Yale Lake Leaf Monster, and Rat King
programme.desc: Whether ISS images signify an imminent alien invasion; whether kayakers encounter a lake monster in Washington or something worse; whether rats are joining forces to create a super rat collective; Tony Harris and his team dive into these claims.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Witch's Broomstick, UFOs vs. Volcanoes, and Oakville Blobs
programme.desc: Is a witch's broomstick floating in midair the real deal or just an elaborate Halloween hoax? Are UFOs using volcanoes as hidden bases of operations? Do fossilized footprints found in Texas prove that dinosaurs and humans walked the earth together?

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A giant carving of a strange creature appears in the Australian outback, and strange bright green lights illuminate the seas around Thailand.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Strange black holes appear near Egypt's pyramids, ruins in Peru suggest the Incas knew more than expected and a mysterious red swarm invades a beach in India.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Legends of ancient giants surround a mysterious monument in the Holy Land. Plus, when a boy disappears, satellite images help with the investigation.

programme.title: Secrets of the Lost Liners
programme.desc: The Titanic sank with a terrible death toll in 1912, but World War I soon overshadowed the tragedy. Decades later, two men strive to rediscover the ship's memory.

programme.title: Secrets Of The Lost Liners
programme.desc: In January 2012, on the first leg of a cruise around the Mediterranean Sea, the Costa Concordia deviated from her route and was rocked by a deadly crash.

programme.title: Hazardous History With Henry Winkler
programme.desc: Ever taken medical advice from a cigarette commercial? There was a time when any and all cures could be sold to the public indiscriminately. Dive into a world where animal skin could replace your own, and where doctors gave coffee enemas.

programme.title: Hitler's Handmaidens
programme.desc: A look at the women who surrounded Hitler after he came to power and how they were held up as an Aryan ideal for German society to strive for once the Nazi dictator approved of their positions.

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Sex Life
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler had a depraved sex life that he tried to hide from the German public. He was said to constantly masturbate, to be a voyeur, and to have sadomasochistic sex with women.

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath investigates and explores the strange legends, unsolved mysteries, bizarre history, unusual traditions and eccentric people of 'Weird Britain'.

programme.title: Weird Britain

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: The tourist-attracting waters of Crater Lake National Park may also be the epicenter of dark and sinister forces from Native American lore that lure visitors to their deaths.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: Claims of a demonic truck driver, disembodied voices, nightmarish skinwalkers and a time loop are reported in Canyonlands National Park.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: In Brazil, three men escape a close encounter only to realise it lasted longer than any of them thought. Plus, in Canada, a boy investigates a strange object.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.sub-title: Bogged Down and Rows and Flows of Angel Hair
programme.desc: In Michigan, a father and son see strange lights and call the sheriff's office. In France, a craft known as Angel Hair is spotted hovering over school grounds.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: On Halloween night in 2004 the police receive over 50 UFO reports in the Chicago area. And in Buck's County, a contractor sees a large object pass over his home.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: US Air Force reservists are pursued by lights while flying back to their base. Plus, constable Godfrey loses consciousness when he investigates a strange disruption.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: A hitchhiker spots a pattern of lights and is terrified when they seem to attack him. A sighting in France becomes one of the most investigated cases in UFO history.

programme.title: Close Encounters
programme.desc: Strange light patterns are seen across multiple counties in Texas. In France, after an intense encounter, a man's previous knee injury is completely healed.

programme.title: Colourblocks
programme.desc: The Colourblocks attempt to solve a rainbow puzzle with the help of new friends Indigo and Violet. Learn about the seven colours of the rainbow with the Colourblocks.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona tries to find a place for a noisy little starling to sleep.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are performing a puppet show for Flop, Amma and Pando when Coco and Charlie arrive. Coco wants to join in with her Rainbow Fairy Mice. Sula tells a frustrated Bing that you have to be loud when you do a show. But Bing is a little too loud, and when Coco joins the show, Bing's loud Biteysaurus isn't welcome, as he is hurting Sula's ears!

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A mysterious giant bunny delays the nighttime picnic.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: The Piggies go camping with Keith and Rebecca and build a great campsite. But at night, strange noises scare everyone, until they find out it's just Wilf, who is even more scared than them!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas help a Bokchoydactyl retrieve its egg from a nest of Cucumberdiles.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Lemon with his laugh-out-loud stand-up act about all things green (and yellow).

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Tao asks Mojo and Bo to help him film an action-packed snowboarding video, but Mojo's not so steady on the slopes. Which top can they swap to grab the white-hot snow shots?

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Rattling Rivets! The Spooky Chugger has returned! With unexplained happenings around Chuggington, the trainees are convinced the tracks are haunted.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea lures Supertato and Tomato into her cave of frosted doom, and they must find the courage to avoid her traps and retrieve The Lost Candle of Birthdays for Tomato's Museum.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: 'You can't stop the bangs but you can help them be quiet.' Sula and Amma go round to Bing's house to watch the fireworks.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula and Amma surprise Bing to show him Sula's sparkly painted butterfly face. Bing also wants a painted face, one that looks like Biteysaurus, and Sula says she can do it for him. Sula begins smothering his face in Bitey's signature green, but when she accidentally adds purple spots, Bing is horrified! He wants Sula to go home. But then Flop adds some teeth to Bing's Biteysaurus, so he can really be Bitey.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are looking for Duggee but can't find him. Instead, they find someone they think is Enid, only much too big... Duggee has painted his face to look like Enid! Of course, the Squirrels want their faces painted too, so Duggee sets to it.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When Duggee's old friend Rhodri goes home after a visit, he forgets his favourite hat. No worries, the Squirrels can run after him and give it back. They take all sorts of transport to catch Rhodri, and he is always one step ahead.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars and Glitch team up and build a fruit-picking machine that runs on methane to help with the Academy harvest. When they run low on fuel, they head to the natural methane deposits of Azerbaijan to stock up. But when the Grimbots take too much methane from a huge volcano, the Go Jetters must work to protect the tourists and save the Grimbots from a fiery explosion.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Fierce Bad Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter and his friends set out to taste the sweetest dandelions in the world, only to find that they're protected by the meanest character in the entire valley!

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When surfing snails are swept out to sea, it's up to Dela and the Octonauts to save them.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Otto keeps getting cramp while teaching dance class. Vida teaches him that he needs to stretch before and after exercise, and pretty soon he's dancing the day away - and so is the rest of Sweetwood!

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs rescuing from a stuck cable car. When the car suddenly starts moving again, Mojo has to ride the cable to chase it down and save poor Preston.

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: After the Back-to-School Challenge you'll be buzzing for the new school year. Sometimes, it's hard to focus, but this challenge is the perfect brain break. Smash through arm circles, push-ups and star jumps, all the way to the Activate!

programme.title: Something Special: We're All Friends
programme.desc: Today, Mr Tumble has invited Lord Tumble and Aunt Polly for tea. Meanwhile, Justin has been invited to a Caribbean restaurant for his tea!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: The family play a game where they trek around the Blue Mountains and outsmart the cheeky fox.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Dad's accused of fluffing in Bluey's face as he climbs out of bed, he's put on trial with Mum as the judge, and Bluey and Bingo as witnesses. Bluey might also have to explain what she was doing there.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are out walking around in the rain with Duggee and notice a rainbow. But before they know it, it's gone and the Squirrels are devastated. They decide to make their own rainbow and go off in search of all the colours from everything around them, including strawberries, carrots and bananas. Duggee shows the Squirrels what he has been working on too, a rainbow cake.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are at the Clubhouse playing, but they can't stop thinking about the dreams they had last night. Luckily, Duggee has his dream badge and can help the Squirrels work out what they mean. As the Squirrels think about dreams, all the different dreams of the Clubhouse friends become weirder and more wonderful. We even get to see Duggee's dream.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Zog cures Fifi's hiccups with a big dragon roar. When patients are too scared to be treated by him, Zog has to choose between being a scary dragon or a doctor.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: When the Emperor Penguin visits the berg, he discovers Pancake is a long-lost Prince. Pancake learns how to act royal, but all the attention goes to his head.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi loves bouncing on the trampoline and wishes she could keep bouncing all day. Her feet become bouncy like trampolines, and at first it feels brilliant. But bouncing everywhere makes simple jobs tricky - cups topple over, eggs splatting, and even hugs are hard. Rafi doesn't want help, but soon she sees that bouncing all the time isn't as fun as she thought. With her dads, she realises it's okay to ask for help. Together they share pancakes, laughter, and a big upside-down hug.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy won't return a video game to the Ducklings until he wins.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Spidey and His Amazing Friends
programme.desc: Zola steals the Bookmobile along with the acclaimed the writer Millicent McKeen. The villains team up to steal back their old inventions from the museum's new exhibit.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Butterflies on Stanley's solar panels mean he has no power for his vehicles or his Can-Bots!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea wears a giant powersuit so powerful it knocks Supertato across the supermarket aisles. Carrot and Supertato plan to outsmart Evil Pea so she'll lose her new powers.

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: The Heroes find themselves battling a rogue plant.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. Ruby sings a diva style ballad about the thing she loves more than anything else, school.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It is picnic day! Rebecca and Murphy craft a 'soup-er' fly in a bowl of soup. Meanwhile, on the Recycling Express, they transform an old storage tub into a terrific drum kit.

programme.title: Big Cook Little Cook
programme.desc: Jen is doing the washing up, but her hands are going wrinkly. Gabby the goalkeeper visits the cafe, and the two chefs make a power shake to give her an energy boost for her next game of football.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through town to water the flowers. On the way, they spot some horses, pretend to be ducks, look at colours in the sweet shop window and walk like signposts!

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: Dr Ollie forgets who he asked to play the wizard in his play! Ava's memory-jogging machine helps him recall, but things get confusing at the nursery.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Ethel slips up and lands with a very big bump. Then it's Naughty Monkey's turn to fall down. The squirrels get Duggee and together they go to help Naughty Monkey, but they have to be very careful.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: As Tweak tests the Gup-X deep down in the Midnight Zone, a yeti crab damages the ship, endangering itself and the Octonauts.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Zigzag hasn't had a poo in two days and doesn't want to go on the scavenger hunt with her friends. Vida sets up a scavenger hunt just for her in the clinic, which is full of clues to help her go.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters swing into action when Glitch uses real landmarks for his crazy golf course.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi makes gingerbread people for the bakery but thinks they look messy next to Jake's neat ones. She wishes them to be perfect - and suddenly they spring to life! The cheeky gingerbreads dash through the café, splattering icing and riding on Deborah's back as they try to reach Gingerbread Land. With Jake's help, Rafi traps them under a cake dome. Dash reminds her that their messy look is what makes them special.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Yukee's bouncing ball sets off a musical chain of events without anyone saying a word!

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: The Froglets explore Lilly's desk then set sail on a daring pirate quest for treasure.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble needs to give Tumble Ted a bubble bath after a jammy mishap at breakfast. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends have lots of fun blowing bubbles.

programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Addi, Elodie, Vincent, Arrella and Harry, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too. The What's in the Box? game reveals a superhero mask, and when the toddlers and parents open up a bigger box, they discover lots more objects and toys that are inspired by superheroes.

programme.title: What's in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Chilli farmer Amrit Madhoo and the Fact Pack explore the colourful world of chillies. They discover how they grow, how ladybirds help protect the plants, and why not all chillies are spicy.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Kyra and Khloe plan a visit to Nana and make chocolate crispy cakes to surprise her with. Visiting Nana is a special occasion, so Mum draws pretty patterns on Kyra and Khloe's hands using henna.

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Butterflies on Stanley's solar panels mean he has no power for his vehicles or his Can-Bots!

programme.desc: Dante's Peak has just been pronounced the second best place in the world to live. Will this title be affected by the danger of the volcano which looms over it? programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Fleming, a Danish restaurant in Miami, Florida. It was once the best family-run restaurant in town, but now the place is on its last legs. Fleming was bought by husband and wife team Andy and Suzanne eight years ago, in the hope of continuing the restaurant's past success, but with no clue about Danish cuisine it's no real surprise the place is losing money. Can Gordon save their bacon?

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is surprised when Phoebe's husband - a gay Canadian ice dancer - shows up, as nobody knew she was married. Elsewhere, Chandler reveals his third nipple, and Ross' new relationship is under attack, unbeknown to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Five Steaks and an Eggplant

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby on the Bus
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler babysit Ben, who turns out to be a babe-magnet until they accidentally leave him on a bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her job as the singer at Central Perk to Stephanie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross and Julie announce they are getting a cat together, while Rachel gets drunk on a date and all she talks about is Ross. She decides to get closure by calling him and telling his machine she is over him. Meanwhile, Chandler regrets asking Monica to help him lose weight.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross agonises over whether he should stay with Julie or date Rachel. The gang encourage him to compare the two, so he starts with a list of Rachel's negative qualities. Unfortunately, Rachel sees the list.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds out that her picture of her father, whom she has never known, is just a generic photograph that comes with the picture frame. Elsewhere, after Ross makes Monica's heater malfunction, her Christmas party guests swelter in the middle of winter.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey gets a great acting opportunity on `Days of our Lives', Rachel dates a guy called Russ who is amazingly similar to Ross, and Monica renews her relationship with `Fun' Bobby, but finds out Bobby is only fun when he is drinking.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Lesbian Wedding
programme.desc: Rachel's free-spirited mother visits and reveals that she wants to end her marriage and experiment sexually. Meanwhile, Carol and Susan prepare for their wedding, with some help from an uncomfortable Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Joey starts dating a fan who is gorgeous, but soon discovers that she's also quite mad. Meanwhile, Ross is reminded how much he misses Marcel his old pet monkey, and travels to San Diego Zoo to find out what has happened to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross's New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Ross comes back from China with his new girlfriend Julie, and Rachel has a really hard time dealing with it. She winds up sleeping with Paolo and hates herself for it. Joey persuades her to tell Ross how she feels, but when Ross tells her he is in love with Julie, she can't bring herself to do it.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Breast Milk

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Absurd comedy about a likeable buffoon who turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment. Mr Bean rushes to his exam only to find out he's studied the wrong subject. He then goes to the beach but struggles to change into his swimming trunks discretely. Later on, he has difficulty attending a church service without drawing attention to himself.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean wants to give a busker some money until he realises he has no change, while afterwards he goes shopping but gets carried away. Later, he treats himself to a birthday dinner but is disappointed with his dish. Finally, Mr Bean attends a royal film premiere in order to meet the Queen, with disastrous results.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes to a swimming pool but finds himself terrified of the high diving board; afterwards, he must try several imaginative ways to get out of the car park when he doesn't have enough money to pay. He then demonstrates the proper way to make a sandwich whilst out at the park. Finally, when Mr Bean and his girlfriend go on a date to watch a horror movie he teases her for being scared, until the film actually starts.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean buys a portable television but can't get it to work properly. Then, when his camera is stolen, he must identify the suspect in a police line up. Afterwards, he attends a magic show and disco with his girlfriend but ends up embarrassing her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Underdog Gets Away
programme.desc: Monica's first Thanksgiving dinner for the gang goes horribly wrong when they all get locked on the roof after watching a giant runaway balloon from the New York Thanksgiving parade. Elsewhere, Joey wins his first modelling gig as the poster boy for a venereal disease campaign, and Ross competes with his ex-wife to communicate with their unborn baby.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: As New Year approaches, the gang make a pact to celebrate at Monica and Rachel's flat without bringing any dates. However, as the New Year gets closer, all of them find themselves breaking that promise except for Ross, who brings along his new pet monkey Marcel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler's flamboyant mother visits New York, while Phoebe and Monica flirt with a handsome stranger while crossing a road and end up causing a traffic accident.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Dozen Lasagnes
programme.desc: Rachel's Italian boyfriend tries to seduce Phoebe, Rachel accidentally reveals a secret to Ross, and Monica's aunt refuses to take the lasagnes she ordered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler sees Rachel nude, and Joey learns that his father is having an affair.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Candy Hearts
programme.desc: It's Valentine's Day and, out on his first date in nine years, Ross meets up with his ex-wife. Meanwhile, Monica, Phoebe and Rachel rebel against the spirit of the day by burning mementos of past relationships.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Stoned Guy
programme.desc: The owner of a fancy restaurant where Monica applies for the chef's position is a little high on illicit substances when he tries the gourmet feast she prepares for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe doesn't get on with her identical twin Ursula. Unfortunately, no one can tell them apart, which leads to a few cases of mistaken identity, not least for Joey who is quite taken with Ursula.

programme.title: Dr. Dolittle 2
programme.desc: A group of beavers, headed by a Godfather-style leader, ask Dolittle to save their habitat from loggers. The only hope is to get the forest preserved because it's the home of a protected bear, but there's a problem: the bear's the only bear in the forest, so she can't reproduce. Undaunted, Dolittle persuades a circus bear to help out, but he has to teach him not just the ways of the wild, but the wiles of lady bears too.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh are back on the road for another epic road trip, and this time they're in for a real culture shock as they head further afield to Latin America. The boys are in Mexico, where they take on Lucha Libre wresting.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes continue their Latin American road trip. They're still in Mexico and on the side of a mountain - we rejoin them to see if Bradley can complete the final part of a challenge which requires him to swing across the top of the mountain with nothing but a terrifying drop below him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After weeks of procrastinating, Rachel finally takes down the Christmas lights, but sprains her ankle.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Poker

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Monkey Gets Away
programme.desc: Rachel accidentally lets Ross' pet monkey escape, and the friends form a search party. She is then horrified to learn that her former best friend is engaged to marry her ex-fiancé.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Evil Orthodontist
programme.desc: Rachel gets back together with her ex-fiancé Barry, even though he's engaged to her best friend Mindy. Meanwhile, Chandler agonises over a wonderful woman who fails to call him after their first date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fake Monica
programme.desc: Someone is pretending to be Monica, using her credit card numbers and having the sort of fun that Monica wishes she could have.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ick Factor
programme.desc: Monica discovers that she's developed a highly mature approach to her latest love affair, and Ross finds out that he's being mistaken for a male prostitute.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Carol gives birth, Ross and Susan almost miss the event when Phoebe accidentally locks them in a cupboard together to put a stop to their constant bickering. Meanwhile, Joey befriends an unmarried pregnant woman and ends up helping her to give birth.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Three contestants hope to answer their way to a fortune, guided by seven celebrity experts - but can they help them to win life-changing cash? That all depends on the spin of The Wheel. The celebrity experts are Anita Rani on Women, Babatunde Aleshe on Eddie Murphy, Ekin-Su on Beauty, Gyles Brandreth on King Charles III, Josh Widdicombe on Train Stations, Nick Knowles on DIY and Stacey Dooley on Strictly.

programme.title: The Waterboy
programme.desc: A lowly water boy for a local college football team is the butt of everyone's jokes until a coach realises that he might just be the team's next star player.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: A topical panel show based on a huge series of opinion polls carried out around Britain. These `Cat Polls' are primed to root out the important issues of the day, like what is Britain's most unwanted wedding gift or who the British public would most like to have round for dinner. Guests David Hasselhoff, Rachel Riley, Rob Beckett and Justin Moorhouse join the regulars.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr delves into the world of opinion polls with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson who are joined by guests Johnny Vegas, Vernon Kay, Paul Chowdhry and Katherine Ryan.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?

programme.desc: When downstairs neighbour Mr Heckles dies, the friends clean out his apartment, but Chandler becomes convinced his hyper-critical nature will mean he ends up alone and sad, just like Heckles.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: World's Greatest Motorcycle Rides
programme.desc: The 1500 mile journey of extremes takes in the Joshua Tree National Park, Death Valley, Las Vegas, Route 66 and the glorious mountain ride through Sedona to journey's end just south of Phoenix Arizona. This once in a lifetime ride into this barren yet unendingly beautiful landscape will etch itself permanently on your soul.

programme.title: World's Greatest Motorcycle Rides
programme.sub-title: America's Longest Highway: Boston to Washington DC
programme.desc: In this the first leg of his ride Henry reflects on the culture and history of Boston before heading off down US 1 into New York, a ride not for the faint hearted. After enjoying the vibe of New York Henry heads south through Philadelphia and Washington DC, before heading into the sublime riding offered up by North and South Carolina.

programme.title: World's Greatest Motorcycle Rides
programme.sub-title: America's Longest Highway: Washington DC to Key West
programme.desc: In the second part of Henry Cole's epic motorcycle ride down the length of America's east coast riding US Highway 1 from Boston to Key West saddle sore and emotional Henry leaves South Carolina and crosses the state line into Georgia revelling in a little light relief offered up by the Laurel and Hardy convention in Harlem but the satire is short lived as the weather closes in.

programme.desc: The honeymoons are coming to an end, and normal life is looming. And that's really quite daunting for some of the less lovey-dovey couples.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica's moodiness puts the family roller-skating tradition at risk, while Beverly takes Adam school clothes shopping.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry goes to work at the furniture store with Murray. Adam and Pops struggle to keep the fact that they watched an R-rated movie from Beverly.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Sal's Pizza, one of Jake Peralta's favourite haunts, burns down, the fire marshal assumes that the owner is the prime suspect in the investigation. Outraged, Jake does everything in his power to prove him wrong.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Laid-back Liam plans to wow his bride with a wintry white wedding. But 2000 miles away in Portugal, bride-to-be Georgia is dreaming of sun, sand and sangria.

programme.title: LEGO Masters: Australia vs The World
programme.desc: From SpongeBob to Baywatch, the six teams recreate an instantly recognisable scene from an iconic TV show. But who'll be a ratings flop?

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: On beautiful Borneo, the celebs get hands-on gutting fish and dive headfirst into a conservation mission. But saving the planet takes a toll on our eco warriors.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: I Do, Deep in the Heart of Austin
programme.desc: Ten singles from Austin, Texas marry complete strangers. As the first two couples wed, there's a shock awaiting one newlywed.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: Don't Mess with My Texas Wedding
programme.desc: Four more singles meet their matches at the altar. And it's not the first time some of them have said 'I do'.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of an explosive two-part special, Gordon tries to turn around the fortunes of Los Angeles restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. It's only been open for 16 months but has already changed its menu more than 10 times, as well as going through 10 chefs and 10 waitresses, and owner Alan has spent his son's inheritance on the restaurant without permission, leading to much family conflict.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The explosive drama continues as Gordon is still in Los Angeles struggling to bring together the fragmented Saffron family and their failing restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Last time, son Daniel nearly came to blows with the head chef, and Gordon was shocked to learn that dad Alan had taken from Daniel's inheritance, without his knowledge, to help fund the start-up of the restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Orange County, Southern California, to try to save an Italian restaurant from imminent destruction. Luigi's was a successful restaurant with a great reputation until Dominic decided to retire to Italy and brought in son Tony to help run it. Familial fighting and an unappetising menu of 126 items have let the restaurant down the drain. Can Gordon fix it, or is Luigi's beyond repair?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Atlanta, Gordon helps first-time restaurant owners Richard and Jorge. Their restaurant, Park's Edge, has been open for three years but is in big trouble due to constant fighting between them, poor food and a bad vibe in the tight-knit neighbourhood. Gordon comes up with a brilliant plan to resolve the issue within the community, but then a rogue chef threatens to ruin everything.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer trashes a marina, he is forced to pay off his debt to the locals by becoming a fisherman. His sea legs are put to the test when a storm strikes his boat.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Revenge Is a Dish Best Served Three Times
programme.desc: Homer is accused of treason in post-revolutionary France, Milhouse leads a nerd rebellion, and mysterious crimefighter `Red Revenge' takes to the streets.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart finds himself tied down with a baby on the way when he falls for Natalie Portman.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa accepts an invitation to join a coven of wiccans and soon finds herself the chief witness in their trial for witchcraft. Meanwhile, Homer starts to hang out with his new pal Cletus after discovering the slack-jawed yokel makes moonshine.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After meeting his millionaire half-brother, Homer sets out to design the perfect car.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Abe tells Marshall Goldman of the time he was on a World War II battleship and the story is later published in the Springfield Shopper. As a result, his Springfield popularity increases rapidly and he decides to `dump' Homer as his son.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Springfield
programme.desc: Krusty's show is losing its female ratings and his executives implement the hire of new female co-star, Princess Penelope. Meanwhile, Mr Burns takes away the free doughnuts from the nuclear power plant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer wins the lottery after skipping a date with Marge. Fearing Marge's rejection, he keeps his winnings a secret while showering his family with anonymous gifts.

programme.title: I, Robot
programme.desc: Set in 2035, Chicago cop Del Spooner doesn't trust the robots that are part of human society. They're supposed to be harmless but Spooner's latest murder hunt unearths a frightening android conspiracy. Only the robotics expert Susan Calvin can help him fight the growing danger, but first he has to convince her that her precious robots can do harm.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britain's best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Part-time wrestler Lewis, who's 19, returns to the First Dates restaurant for a second chance at finding love. Meanwhile, can hotel inspector Alasdair and fun-loving DJ Lee hit all the right notes as they share their love of the pop parade?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Can laboratory engineer Daniel, who's 39, get his first date nerves under control when he meets 29-year-old lingerie designer Lauren? Call centre worker Zoe, who's 21, is looking for an intelligent and ladylike bookworm.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Northamptonshire, loved-up luvvies Anna and Coylah, sporty Felicity and Lawrence and well-to-do Martin and Dawn compete in a week featuring unthawed prawns, a panto, and limelight theft.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Drag Queens
programme.desc: Dan tries to plan the perfect day for his partner Mark. Despite being engaged, their plans have been on ice due to lack of cash, but now Mark has decided he's going to hand over control of his big day to Dan. Will best man Steve derail the wedding plans?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Newquaye Laura and Mitch are desperate to walk down the aisle, but there's another love in Mitch's life, one that threatens to come between Laura and her dream wedding. Surf addict Mitch must plan a wedding with one eye permanently on the waves.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in a Boarding School
programme.desc: Bedfordshire couple Mercy and James have been together for 13 years and got engaged in 2009. Mercy comes from a Nigerian background, so it is a matter of family pride that James gets the traditions just right. How will this most laidback of grooms know what colour tribal outfits to buy and will his lack of planning undo his `I Do'?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Kidderminster country boy Nathan attempts to blow away bride-to-be Nicky with his unique take on a farm wedding. They met seven years ago at a nightclub in Dudley and have been inseparable ever since, but Nathan is not a born romantic. Will he be a fish out of water in the wonderful world of bridal dresses and women's lingerie?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: After just 11 months together, Hywel proposed to Beca at a Blur gig. This trainee social worker wants to rock her world as he tries to bring Glastonbury to Bridgend for their wedding day.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Shane Evans is COO and co-founder of Massage Heights, one of the biggest massage therapy chains in North America. She goes undercover posing as a participant in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to fund her own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Bruce `Two Dogs' Bozsum of Mohegan Sun, a Native American gaming and entertainment empire owned and operated by the Mohegan tribe of Connecticut, goes undercover posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon's in Redondo Beach, California, to visit Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant owned and run by aunt and niece duo Faye and Brenda.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is back in Redondo Beach, California, for the conclusion of his challenge to save Zayna Flaming Grill from imploding, and he still faces more questions than answers.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Easton, Pennsylvania, to visit struggling Italian restaurant Bella Luna. Rosaria bought the establishment to realise her son Gianfranco's ambition to become a chef.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Ramsay revisits Boston's La Galleria, the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Mass., and the Prohibition Gastropub in Everett, Wash.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels in the USA, aiming to fix hopeless hotels, mediocre motels and bed & breakfasts with bad standards. In the first programme, Gordon visits Coeur d'Alene, Idaho, to try to rescue the failing Roosevelt Inn. Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the Roosevelt's owner, John Hough, is more interested in telling jokes and dressing up as Sherlock Holmes than he is in fixing his struggling B&B.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with `Countdown' regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones. Mr Swallow, aka Nick Mohammed, is in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with Countdown regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent as Sarah Millican and Nish Kumar take on Jon Richardson and Maisie Adam. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum are in search of alien life, they're heading to the UFO capital of the USA: Nevada. They join an abductee support group and venture out to a UFO hot spot.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum goes on an eye-opening trip around the USA, meeting an array of weird and wonderful characters.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: An extended edition with host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson. They are joined by celebrity guests Jo Brand, Nick Helm, Jordan from the Rizzle Kicks and Louis Walsh.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr is joined by team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson for an episode of the best bits from series 16. A host of well-known faces join the teams as they discuss the topical events that set the great British public's chins wagging.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with `Countdown' regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones. Mr Swallow, aka Nick Mohammed, is in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Patsy Palmer from `EastEnders' loves a good wedding and she has picked her top 20 most jaw-dropping moments from the first three series of `Don't Tell the Bride'.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Payback
programme.desc: In three years of `Don't Tell the Bride', dozens of brave brides have handed complete control of the biggest day of their lives to their blokes. In return, their grooms were given £12,000 and a chance to prove themselves to the woman they love. Did the blushing brides get the wedding they'd always dreamed of? The girls take the chance to have their say.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Skydiving Bride
programme.desc: John and his fiancée Jackie live in Kirkintilloch, an old mining town on the outskirts of Glasgow. Country boy John was born there and has never left, but city girl Jackie hails from Glasgow's West End and has always dreamt of a sophisticated and glamorous wedding. John has his head in the clouds for his bride's big day and wants to throw Jackie out of a plane at 10,000 feet on the morning of her wedding.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Joe DiDomizio is the president and CEO of Hudson Group, a travel retailer that operates newsstands, bookshops, cafés and speciality stores in 70 airports and other transportation terminals throughout North America.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Utah Jazz CEO Greg Miller's team spirit is tested when he is asked to be a human basketball hoop while he is undercover.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First up in the West Midlands are free spirits Michelle and Carl, who get all Zen over their home-grown produce. They're joined by Katie and Phil, and Petra and her Italian husband Sergio.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mike and Lisa kick off the competition in Worcester with an Italian-themed menu. Linda and Steve opt for a completely homemade menu, and married couple Paul and Paul take a risk.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottingham, long-time married couple Karen and Nigel kick off the competition with a Bajan themed menu inspired by Nigel's West Indian heritage. Joining them at the table are twenty-somethings Beth and Clem, and banter buddies Jo and Charlie.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Cambridge, New York, where ex-military man John Imhof and his wife, Tina, own and operate the allegedly haunted Cambridge Hotel.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: The first part of a two-part visit to the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont, a place that looks like a million dollars but smells of raw sewage. Gordon meets the inn's wealthy owner, Robert Dean II, and uncovers some shocking treatment towards the staff. Gordon quickly realises that it's going to need something approaching a miracle if he's going to have a chance of saving the place.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: This programme sees Mr Ramsay's greatest challenge to date, as he continues to uncover problem after problem at the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont. He tries to help the staff communicate with owners Robert and Ari, but after catching one of them in a shocking lie, Gordon feels he has no choice but to abandon ship.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay checks in to the River Rock Inn in Milford, Pennsylvania. Owner Ken Pisciotta is in over his head and drowning in debt and unable to get any guests to stay in his neglected establishment.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits The Keating, a `luxury' boutique hotel in San Diego, California. Owner, Eddie Kaen, has blown millions designing the hotel to look like an Italian sports car, but neglected to spend money on things the guests really need.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Armando Iannucci and Joanna Page contemplate an alien invasion in the Taskmaster house. Joel Dommett enjoys putting things on things and Amy Gledhill performs sock-based stand-up.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with `Countdown' regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent as Roisin Conaty and Asim Chaudhry take on Jon Richardson and Josh Widdicombe. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum venture south to the Hip Hop Capital of the USA.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum head to one of the USA's most haunted cities: Savannah, Georgia.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Christmas special with host Jimmy Carr, captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock, and guests Holly Willoughby, Henning Wehn, Alan Davies and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm and Joe Wilkinson also make guest appearances.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr, team captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock, and celebrity guests discuss and dissect the topical events that set the great British public's chins wagging.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with `Countdown' regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent as Roisin Conaty and Asim Chaudhry take on Jon Richardson and Josh Widdicombe. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Christmas special with host Jimmy Carr, captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock, and guests Holly Willoughby, Henning Wehn, Alan Davies and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm and Joe Wilkinson also make guest appearances.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry goes to work at the furniture store with Murray. Adam and Pops struggle to keep the fact that they watched an R-rated movie from Beverly.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Sal's Pizza, one of Jake Peralta's favourite haunts, burns down, the fire marshal assumes that the owner is the prime suspect in the investigation. Outraged, Jake does everything in his power to prove him wrong.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Laid-back Liam plans to wow his bride with a wintry white wedding. But 2000 miles away in Portugal, bride-to-be Georgia is dreaming of sun, sand and sangria.

programme.title: LEGO Masters: Australia vs The World
programme.desc: From SpongeBob to Baywatch, the six teams recreate an instantly recognisable scene from an iconic TV show. But who'll be a ratings flop?

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: On beautiful Borneo, the celebs get hands-on gutting fish and dive headfirst into a conservation mission. But saving the planet takes a toll on our eco warriors.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: I Do, Deep in the Heart of Austin
programme.desc: Ten singles from Austin, Texas marry complete strangers. As the first two couples wed, there's a shock awaiting one newlywed.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: Don't Mess with My Texas Wedding
programme.desc: Four more singles meet their matches at the altar. And it's not the first time some of them have said 'I do'.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of an explosive two-part special, Gordon tries to turn around the fortunes of Los Angeles restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. It's only been open for 16 months but has already changed its menu more than 10 times, as well as going through 10 chefs and 10 waitresses, and owner Alan has spent his son's inheritance on the restaurant without permission, leading to much family conflict.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The explosive drama continues as Gordon is still in Los Angeles struggling to bring together the fragmented Saffron family and their failing restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Last time, son Daniel nearly came to blows with the head chef, and Gordon was shocked to learn that dad Alan had taken from Daniel's inheritance, without his knowledge, to help fund the start-up of the restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Orange County, Southern California, to try to save an Italian restaurant from imminent destruction. Luigi's was a successful restaurant with a great reputation until Dominic decided to retire to Italy and brought in son Tony to help run it. Familial fighting and an unappetising menu of 126 items have let the restaurant down the drain. Can Gordon fix it, or is Luigi's beyond repair?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Atlanta, Gordon helps first-time restaurant owners Richard and Jorge. Their restaurant, Park's Edge, has been open for three years but is in big trouble due to constant fighting between them, poor food and a bad vibe in the tight-knit neighbourhood. Gordon comes up with a brilliant plan to resolve the issue within the community, but then a rogue chef threatens to ruin everything.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer trashes a marina, he is forced to pay off his debt to the locals by becoming a fisherman. His sea legs are put to the test when a storm strikes his boat.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Revenge Is a Dish Best Served Three Times
programme.desc: Homer is accused of treason in post-revolutionary France, Milhouse leads a nerd rebellion, and mysterious crimefighter `Red Revenge' takes to the streets.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart finds himself tied down with a baby on the way when he falls for Natalie Portman.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa accepts an invitation to join a coven of wiccans and soon finds herself the chief witness in their trial for witchcraft. Meanwhile, Homer starts to hang out with his new pal Cletus after discovering the slack-jawed yokel makes moonshine.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After meeting his millionaire half-brother, Homer sets out to design the perfect car.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Abe tells Marshall Goldman of the time he was on a World War II battleship and the story is later published in the Springfield Shopper. As a result, his Springfield popularity increases rapidly and he decides to `dump' Homer as his son.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Springfield
programme.desc: Krusty's show is losing its female ratings and his executives implement the hire of new female co-star, Princess Penelope. Meanwhile, Mr Burns takes away the free doughnuts from the nuclear power plant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer wins the lottery after skipping a date with Marge. Fearing Marge's rejection, he keeps his winnings a secret while showering his family with anonymous gifts.

programme.title: I, Robot
programme.desc: Set in 2035, Chicago cop Del Spooner doesn't trust the robots that are part of human society. They're supposed to be harmless but Spooner's latest murder hunt unearths a frightening android conspiracy. Only the robotics expert Susan Calvin can help him fight the growing danger, but first he has to convince her that her precious robots can do harm.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britain's best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Part-time wrestler Lewis, who's 19, returns to the First Dates restaurant for a second chance at finding love. Meanwhile, can hotel inspector Alasdair and fun-loving DJ Lee hit all the right notes as they share their love of the pop parade?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Can laboratory engineer Daniel, who's 39, get his first date nerves under control when he meets 29-year-old lingerie designer Lauren? Call centre worker Zoe, who's 21, is looking for an intelligent and ladylike bookworm.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.
